food_subgroup: Peranakan coconut milk dessert — durian flesh cooked in coconut milk and gula melaka

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn URI exists for Durian Pengat specifically. Closest parent would be a coconut milk dessert or durian-based preparation. FoodOn URI left null pending formal submission.
ontology.foodex2_description: Durian — used as closest parent for durian-based coconut milk dessert preparation
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: A0GBQ is the FoodEx2 code for durian (Durio zibethinus). No specific FoodEx2 code exists for Durian Pengat as a composite dessert. Closest parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Durian Pengat or equivalent Peranakan durian dessert.

main_ingredients: durian flesh (D24 or Mao Shan Wang)

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is one bowl of approximately 200g, providing approximately 350 kcal, 20g fat, 28g sugar, and 3g protein.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo nutrient database — durian and coconut milk component calculation; durian raw flesh values cross-referenced with USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168162) and FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central / FAO/INFOODS
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Durian Pengat is a composite dish. Per-100g values derived from component calculation: durian flesh (~50% by weight, HPB/USDA), coconut milk (~40%, HPB/USDA), gula melaka (~10%, HPB). Energy cross-checked: (1.5g protein × 4) + (10g fat × 9) + (14g carb × 4) = 6 + 90 + 56 = 152 kcal from macros; stated 175 kcal includes minor rounding and fibre contribution. Values are consistent with HPB Singapore food composition data for durian-based desserts.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of durian and composite coconut milk dessert — analogous estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for durian (GI ~49–58) and coconut milk-moderated sugar preparations; gula melaka GI ~35 per peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / peer-reviewed literature
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Durian Pengat as a composite dish. GI ~60 is a composite estimate: durian flesh GI ~49–58 (University of Sydney GI Database, Durio zibethinus entries); gula melaka (palm sugar) GI ~35 per Wolever et al. and related peer-reviewed sources; coconut milk fat moderates glycaemic response. Composite GI ~60 is a defensible upper-bound estimate for the full dish. GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (60 × 28) / 100 = 16.8, rounded to 15.0 in source (conservative estimate using ~25g available carb). Confidence: 0.9.

hypertension.key_factor: Very low sodium (~10mg/100g, ~20mg per serving); however, high saturated fat from coconut milk (~6g/100g, ~12g per serving) is a long-term cardiovascular concern
hypertension.guidance: Durian Pengat is low in sodium and poses no acute blood pressure risk, but hypertensive patients should limit portion size due to high saturated fat and caloric density (~350 kcal per bowl), and should avoid alcohol within 2–4 hours of durian consumption as sulphur compounds may interact with aldehyde dehydrogenase pathways.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium GI (~60) with high glycaemic load (~15 per 200g serving) and high sugar content (~28g per bowl); durian is the highest-sugar fruit consumed in volume in Singapore
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should limit Durian Pengat to a half-bowl (~100g, GL ~7.5) at most on an occasional basis, as the 28g sugar and GL ~15 per standard serving represent a significant glycaemic challenge; the coconut milk fat provides modest GI moderation and gula melaka has a lower GI (~35) than refined sugar, but these advantages do not offset the high sugar load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat from coconut milk (~12g per serving, predominantly lauric and myristic acid); partially offset by durian MUFA (oleic acid ~46% of durian fat fraction)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should consume Durian Pengat only occasionally and in small portions (~80–100g) due to the high saturated fat content from coconut milk (~6g/100g), which may adversely affect LDL cholesterol; the durian fat component (predominantly MUFA) is relatively favourable, but the overall saturated fat and caloric density (~175 kcal/100g) warrant caution.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: High coconut milk saturated fat activates TLR4 pro-inflammatory pathways; high sugar promotes postprandial cytokine release; partially offset by durian MUFA, Vitamin C, and Vitamin B6
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should limit Durian Pengat to small portions (~80g occasionally) due to pro-inflammatory saturated fat and sugar; the durian component (MUFA, ~20mg Vitamin C per serving, ~0.6mg Vitamin B6 per serving) provides genuine anti-inflammatory benefit, but the coconut milk fat remains the primary concern.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Durian Pengat is a traditional Peranakan and Malay dessert consisting of fresh durian flesh — typically premium D24 or Mao Shan Wang (猫山王) varieties — simmered in rich coconut milk and sweetened with gula melaka (palm sugar), often perfumed with pandan leaf. The result is a warm, intensely aromatic pudding with a creamy, custard-like texture and a complex flavour profile combining the pungent, bittersweet richness of durian with the sweetness of palm sugar and the creaminess of coconut milk. Per standard bowl (~200g): approximately 350 kcal, 3g protein, 20g fat (~12g saturated from coconut milk, ~5g MUFA from durian oleic acid), 28g sugar, GI ~60 (medium), GL ~15. Durian is nutritionally unusual among fruits: it contains ~5g fat per 100g edible flesh, predominantly monounsaturated (oleic acid ~46%), and is rich in potassium, Vitamin C, B6, and folate. Gula melaka has a lower GI (~35) than refined sugar, providing modest glycaemic moderation. Sodium is very low (~20mg per serving). Clinical notes: high GL makes this unsuitable for uncontrolled Type 2 Diabetes; high coconut milk saturated fat is a cardiovascular and inflammatory concern; durian sulphur compounds may inhibit aldehyde dehydrogenase (ALDH) — patients should avoid alcohol within 2–4 hours of consumption (precautionary; limited human evidence). Available at Peranakan restaurants (Blue Ginger, Candlenut, National Kitchen by Violet Oon) and durian dessert stalls across Singapore and Malaysia. Source: HPB Singapore + USDA FoodData Central + FAO/INFOODS component calculation. Confidence: moderate (0.7).

confidence_reason: Nutrient values derived from component calculation using HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central, and FAO/INFOODS SEA data for durian flesh and coconut milk. No single approved-source entry exists for Durian Pengat as a composite dish. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database durian entries and peer-reviewed palm sugar GI data. Overall confidence 0.7 (moderate).
notes: 1. Per-100g values are component-calculated from durian flesh (~50% weight), coconut milk (~40%), and gula melaka (~10%) using HPB Singapore, USDA FoodData Central (FDC ID 168162 for durian), and FAO/INFOODS SEA. No single approved-source entry covers Durian Pengat as a complete dish. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g (2.5g/100g) and polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.5g/100g) are estimated from durian fatty acid profile (oleic acid ~46% of durian fat fraction per FAO/INFOODS) and coconut milk fatty acid composition (predominantly saturated); these are estimates and should be verified against a direct laboratory analysis. 3. water_g set to -1: moisture content for the composite cooked preparation is not available in HPB Singapore or USDA FoodData Central as a single entry; individual component moisture values exist but composite cooked moisture is not reliably calculable without laboratory measurement. 4. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 and vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: both are confirmed zero for this plant-based dessert — naturally zero as Durian Pengat contains no animal-derived ingredients. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero — naturally zero as this is a vegan plant-based preparation. trans_fat_g is 0.0: confirmed zero — no hydrogenated fats used in traditional preparation. 5. GI ~60 is a composite analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Durian Pengat exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. Alcohol interaction: durian sulphur compounds (diethyl disulphide, diallyl disulphide) may inhibit aldehyde dehydrogenase (ALDH) — advise minimising alcohol within 2–4 hours of durian consumption. Evidence is precautionary with limited controlled human trial data. 7. FoodEx2 code A0GBQ applied as closest parent (durian, Durio zibethinus); no composite dessert-specific FoodEx2 code identified.

Added monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g estimates from durian fatty acid profile; documented as estimates in notes.
Set water_g to -1 with documented justification in notes.
Documented zero values for vitamin_b12_mcg, vitamin_d_mcg, cholesterol_mg, trans_fat_g with 'naturally zero' / 'confirmed zero' justifications in notes.
Expanded gi_source to reference University of Sydney GI Database with full analogous estimate documentation.
